data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update CLAUDE.md to reflect Epic C's CI parity + 3-tier distribution mechanism in the Architecture section.

**Why**: After this epic ships, agents working on the codebase need accurate context about the new CI llm-review architecture, the 3-tier distribution mechanism, and the auto-detect runner mode. CLAUDE.md is loaded into every session — stale content here propagates to every interaction.

**Scope**:
- IN: edit CLAUDE.md Architecture section to describe (a) ci-llm-review-runner.sh as the orchestrator for CI llm-review, (b) the 3-tier version resolution + fetch mechanism for host-project CI, (c) the auto-detect runner mode (local-checkout vs fetched-assets, marker file plugins/dso/.dso-source-of-truth), (d) the deep-tier and overlay parallel dispatch protocol, (e) the divergence from local agent-batch-lifecycle (CI does not consult check-usage.sh).
- OUT: CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md edits (covered by Story 8); creating any new documentation file (CLAUDE.md is the architecture surface).

Follow plugins/dso/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Architecture section contains a concise description of the ci-llm-review-runner.sh orchestrator, the 3-tier version resolution chain, and the auto-detect runner mode.
  ← Satisfies: keep agent context accurate post-epic
- When this story is complete, the description follows the existing CLAUDE.md style (one-line items in the architecture section with one-line cross-references; full implementation details remain in linked SKILL.md / docs files).
  ← Satisfies: "Updates existing docs only — do not create new documentation files or patterns"
- unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic (a structural test that the new architecture lines exist in CLAUDE.md)

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] CLAUDE.md content guidance per existing CLAUDE.md governance: rules, command references, and one-line architecture summaries — NOT verbose implementation details. Long-form architecture details belong in linked SKILL.md / docs files.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: Update project docs (CLAUDE.md) to reflect the parity-uplifted CI llm-review architecture and 3-tier distribution mechanism
